Form language — three candidates (same electronics, different soul)

All keep: no buttons · glow ring · touch dish · liquid port · dock ring + pogo underside · pocket/stick/hang/desk. The Amulet (wearable-first flat drop) remains a described option / future second SKU — not rendered.

Compass Puck — 58 × 58 × 16 mm

iso
profile
plan
  • Use: Round instrument, zero orientation. Hover-to-examine (sensor field on the underside), glow bezel that POINTS toward findings, crown touch dish, liquid port at the south rim. Coin-pocket, dock ring, pocket-watch lanyard.
  • Why it speaks: Instrument gravitas; the pointing bezel is a signature interaction; round board suits radial zones.
  • Trade: Primary gesture is hover/flat-sweep, not aiming across a room.

River Pebble — 76 × 52 × 20 mm

iso
profile
plan
  • Use: Fully symmetric worry-stone (top/bottom AND left/right) — no wrong way to hold it. Edge-band sensors (sweep with the edge), thumb dish on both faces, glow equator seam, liquid dimple at the nose.
  • Why it speaks: The most intimate 'extension of yourself' — a stone you fidget with is already in your hand when you need it.
  • Trade: Least instrument-like; taper is the only aim cue; double-sided symmetry costs interior volume.

The Wand — 95 × 32 × 16 mm

iso
profile
plan
  • Use: Rounded bar with an unapologetic sensor SNOUT: optics/radar/air at the end face, point it like a flashlight. Thumb dish on top, liquid tip under the nose (dip like a pipette), glow keel line.
  • Why it speaks: Clearest point-and-interrogate semantics; professional-tool reading for safety/industrial verticals.
  • Trade: Most gadget-like; long-thin fights the big gas cells; directional rather than orientation-free.

My recommendation

  • Compass Puck for product identity ("a compass for reality") and engineering fit — with the Pebble as the close second if intimacy beats instrument-ness. Print both STLs (enclosure/concept_puck.stl, concept_pebble.stl) and hold them — grip decides, not renders.
